name: Mimung
description: Mimung is a legendary sword in Norse mythology, primarily associated with the Volsung saga. Forged by the renowned smith Völundr (Wayland the Smith), Mimung is said to possess unparalleled sharpness and strength, capable of cleaving through any substance. Its creation is shrouded in mystery, with some accounts suggesting it was imbued with magical properties during its forging. The sword is initially owned by the hero Sigurd's father, Sigmund. Mimung plays a crucial role in the Volsung saga, being broken by Odin himself during a battle between Sigmund and an opposing king. The broken pieces are later reforged by Sigurd, becoming the legendary sword Gram. Mimung's significance lies not only in its exceptional quality but also in its symbolic representation of heroism, fate, and the cyclical nature of power. Its lineage connects it to both the divine craftsmanship of Völundr and the heroic lineage of the Volsungs, solidifying its place as a central artifact in Norse legend. The sword is often interpreted as a symbol of the inherent strength and destiny of the Volsung clan, as well as the tragic fate that often befalls great heroes. Some scholars believe Mimung represents the unbreakable spirit of the Volsungs, even when faced with overwhelming odds and divine intervention. The sword's ultimate fate, reforged as Gram, further emphasizes its importance in shaping the destiny of Sigurd and the events of the saga. The echoes of Mimung resonate throughout the narrative, serving as a constant reminder of the power and peril associated with heroic endeavors.
subtitle: The Sword of the Volsungs
currentLocation: Lost to time, possibly reforged into Gram

keyMyths.title: The Breaking of Mimung
keyMyths.description: During a battle, Odin appears disguised as an old man and shatters Mimung with his spear, causing Sigmund to fall. This marks a turning point in Sigmund's life and sets the stage for Sigurd's birth.

keyMyths.title: The Reforging as Gram
keyMyths.description: The shattered pieces of Mimung are given to Sigurd, who has them reforged by the smith Regin. The reforged sword, now named Gram, becomes Sigurd's legendary weapon, capable of slaying the dragon Fafnir.

sources.author: Unknown
